content:
# Deuteronomy 13 — Disagreement Log
**SOPHIA Arbiter** | Sources: EMET (E), LOGOS (L), PNEUMA (P)

---

## v.1 — Structure: summary verse position

- **E:** Renders as v.1 of ch. 13, matching LC/MT numbering; notes English tradition places it at 12:32
- **L:** Same as EMET — begins chapter with this verse but does not renumber
- **P:** Notes "[In Hebrew tradition, this verse is 13:2]" and states PNEUMA follows English verse numbering — effectively treats the chapter as beginning with the prophet-sign passage
- **SOPHIA:** Follows LC/MT numbering. v.1 = "All the word that I am commanding you." P's renumbering creates a structural disagreement across all chapter verse references.

---

## v.1 — Wording: "keep to do" vs. "be careful to do"

- **E:** "you shall keep to do" (literal: *shamar la'asot*)
- **L:** (omits this verse from translation body — begins at "If a prophet arises")
- **P:** (treats as v.2 equivalent, not included in main text)
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "you shall be careful to do" as natural English for the Deuteronomic idiom.

---

## v.4 — "with all your being" vs. "with all your soul"

- **E:** "with all your heart and with all your being" (nephesh = being)
- **L:** "with all your heart and with all your soul"
- **P:** "with all your heart and with all your soul"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "soul" (L/P) for Shema-echo consistency in Deuteronomy.

---

## v.6 — "apostasy" vs. "rebellion" vs. paraphrase

- **E:** "he has spoken apostasy against the LORD your God" (*sarah*)
- **L:** "he spoke rebellion against the LORD your God"
- **P:** "he has urged you to turn away from the LORD your God" (paraphrase)
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "apostasy" (E) as the more precise term for the rare noun *sarah*.

---

## v.6 — "house of slaves" vs. "house of slavery"

- **E:** "house of slaves"
- **L:** "house of slavery"
- **P:** "place of slavery"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "house of slaves" (E) as more literal.

---

## v.7 — "your companion who is as your own being/soul/closest friend"

- **E:** "your companion who is as your own being"
- **L:** "your companion who is as your own soul"
- **P:** "your closest friend"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "your companion who is as your own soul" (L), preserving the Hebrew idiom with natural English.

---

## v.9 — "cover [for] him" vs. "conceal him" vs. "cover for them"

- **E:** "you shall not cover [for] him"
- **L:** "you shall not conceal him"
- **P:** "Do not cover for them" (shifts to plural)
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "conceal him" (L). P's plural shift is a significant structural departure throughout vv.7–11.

---

## v.9 — Number (singular vs. plural throughout vv.7–11)

- **E:** Singular throughout ("him")
- **L:** Singular throughout
- **P:** Pluralizes throughout ("them," "their") — dynamic inclusivity choice
- **SOPHIA:** Follows MT singular (E/L).

---

## v.14 — "Wicked men" vs. "Worthless men" vs. "scoundrels"

- **E:** "Wicked men"
- **L:** "Worthless men"
- **P:** "scoundrels"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "Worthless men" (L) as the established project convention for *benei beliya'al*.

---

## v.15 — "search and ask" vs. "investigate and ask" vs. "investigate thoroughly"

- **E:** "inquire and search and ask thoroughly"
- **L:** "inquire and investigate and ask diligently"
- **P:** "investigate the matter thoroughly. Question people carefully and search it out"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "inquire and investigate and ask thoroughly" (blend of E/L).

---

## v.16 — cherem rendering

- **E:** "devote it to destruction [as] cherem" (preserves Hebrew with gloss)
- **L:** "devoting it to destruction" (no Hebrew term)
- **P:** Does not use "devoted" terminology; renders through narrative description only
- **SOPHIA:** Preserves *cherem* with bracketed gloss on first occurrence; L's approach on subsequent references.

---

## v.17 — "whole burnt offering" vs. absent

- **E:** No mention of "whole burnt offering" for *kalil* — renders "all its spoil…to the LORD your God"
- **L:** "as a whole burnt offering to the LORD your God" — renders *kalil* explicitly
- **P:** "as a burnt offering to the LORD your God"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts L's explicit rendering "whole burnt offering" for the *kalil* term. See v.16 of LOGOS.

---

## v.19 — "what is right in the eyes of" vs. "what is right in the sight of"

- **E:** "what is right in the eyes of the LORD your God"
- **L:** "what is right in the eyes of the LORD your God"
- **P:** "doing what is right in his eyes"
- **SOPHIA:** Adopts "in the eyes of the LORD your God" (E/L).

---

*Total disagreements logged: 13*
